prs.title: Fixing the f-string which you don,t know Samadhiya
prs.body:
Fixing the f-string which you don,t know Samadhiya
This pull request makes a minor update to the way the `st.markdown` function is called in the `main` function of `app.py`. The change switches from a regular triple-quoted string to an f-string, allowing for inline variable interpolation in the markdown content.

prs.title: Update markdown rendering to use f-strings for dynamic content
prs.body:
This pull request refactors the way HTML is rendered in the Streamlit app by consistently using Python f-strings in all `st.markdown` calls. This change ensures that dynamic values are properly interpolated into the HTML templates, improving maintainability and reducing the risk of rendering issues.

**Refactoring for consistent HTML rendering:**

* All `st.markdown` calls in `app.py` are updated to use f-strings, allowing dynamic variables to be directly embedded in the HTML markup. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-568470d013cd12e4f388206520da39ab9a4e4c3c6b95846cbc281abc1ba3c959L604-R604) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-568470d013cd12e4f388206520da39ab9a4e4c3c6b95846cbc281abc1ba3c959L626-R626) [[3]](diffhunk://#diff-568470d013cd12e4f388206520da39ab9a4e4c3c6b95846cbc281abc1ba3c959L657-R657) [[4]](diffhunk://#diff-568470d013cd12e4f388206520da39ab9a4e4c3c6b95846cbc281abc1ba3c959L750-R750) [[5]](diffhunk://#diff-568470d013cd12e4f388206520da39ab9a4e4c3c6b95846cbc281abc1ba3c959L767-R767) [[6]](diffhunk://#diff-568470d013cd12e4f388206520da39ab9a4e4c3c6b95846cbc281abc1ba3c959L829-R829) [[7]](diffhunk://#diff-568470d013cd12e4f388206520da39ab9a4e4c3c6b95846cbc281abc1ba3c959L862-R862) [[8]](diffhunk://#diff-568470d013cd12e4f388206520da39ab9a4e4c3c6b95846cbc281abc1ba3c959L984-R984) [[9]](diffhunk://#diff-568470d013cd12e4f388206520da39ab9a4e4c3c6b95846cbc281abc1ba3c959L1007-R1014)

prs.title: fix: Update render_metric to use f-string for dynamic content
prs.body:
Aaditya mohan if you have common sense you can fix this issues and its not my fault ## before ``` def render_metric(label, value, color_class="glow-text", icon=""): st.markdown("""
{icon} {label}
{value}
""", unsafe_allow_html=True) ``` ## after ``` def render_metric(label, value, color_class="glow-text", icon=""): st.markdown(f"""
{icon} {label}
{value}
""", unsafe_allow_html=True) ``` You just need to use common sense and f-string

chore: Refactor dependency installation to use requirements-test.txt …
prs.body:
This pull request improves dependency management for the testing workflow by consolidating all test-related Python dependencies into a single requirements file and updating the CI configuration to use this file. This change simplifies dependency installation and makes it easier to maintain consistency across development and CI environments.

Dependency management improvements:

* Added a new `requirements-test.txt` file listing all test and core dependencies required for running tests, including frameworks, API tools, and RAG pipeline libraries.
* Updated the `.github/workflows/ci.yml` CI workflow to install dependencies using `pip install -r requirements-test.txt` instead of multiple individual `pip install` commands.

prs.title: fix: Update markdown rendering in main app and improve error message …
prs.body:
This pull request introduces several improvements and refactorings to both the application code (`app.py`) and the test suite (`tests/test_retriever.py`). The main focus is on enhancing code readability and maintainability by reducing inline logic in markdown strings, introducing intermediate variables, and improving test robustness for optional dependencies.

**Key changes:**

### Code readability and maintainability improvements (`app.py`):

* Refactored multiple `st.markdown` calls to use f-strings with precomputed variables (such as `mileage_k`, `error_msg`, `risk_level`, `days_range`, and `downtime_cost`) instead of inline expressions, making the code easier to read and maintain. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-568470d013cd12e4f388206520da39ab9a4e4c3c6b95846cbc281abc1ba3c959L657-R657) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-568470d013cd12e4f388206520da39ab9a4e4c3c6b95846cbc281abc1ba3c959L726-R730) [[3]](diffhunk://#diff-568470d013cd12e4f388206520da39ab9a4e4c3c6b95846cbc281abc1ba3c959L767-R773) [[4]](diffhunk://#diff-568470d013cd12e4f388206520da39ab9a4e4c3c6b95846cbc281abc1ba3c959R850-R867)

### Testing robustness improvements (`tests/test_retriever.py`):

* Updated the `test_retrieve_large_k` test to explicitly check for the presence of the `faiss` library and skip the test if it is not installed, ensuring the test suite behaves predictably in different environments. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-0a16a9b3407731d626139acc756b92c6edd5cede97b04445f9fa3d5a2b0ebe63R162-R166) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-0a16a9b3407731d626139acc756b92c6edd5cede97b04445f9fa3d5a2b0ebe63L179-L186)

prs.title: Refactor imports and clean up code across multiple modules for improv…
prs.body:
This pull request primarily focuses on code cleanup and minor improvements across several modules, including removing unused imports, improving code clarity, and making some small formatting changes. These updates enhance code readability and reduce unnecessary dependencies, especially in test files and utility scripts.

**Code cleanup and import management:**

* Removed unused imports (such as `pytest`, `Optional`, `json`, and others) from multiple files including `chatbot/memory.py`, `chatbot/vehicle_ai.py`, `main.py`, and several test files to streamline the codebase and avoid linter warnings. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-e5b8c4ce9695f8b74c8af6babe6cb83019ac288de0bacff9f9e506a86366d34cL5) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-2a1f860593a5339f223f4bc83cd5e61060de8c162fb42e0f13f681ad121faccdL8-L9) [[3]](diffhunk://#diff-b10564ab7d2c520cdd0243874879fb0a782862c3c902ab535faabe57d5a505e1L4-L8) [[4]](diffhunk://#diff-3451cc474d5698222168f66a293339a6bd296ee2c56f56409866bbae99917a79L5) [[5]](diffhunk://#diff-3b181fe4fec2a18cea98e392564d73c779b3b9f5de805c55fafbb81689a623d8L4-L8) [[6]](diffhunk://#diff-2c8332d3e78b0d70e15aa7cbea0912043786c9dfe01a0963d04552c68637dc3bL4) [[7]](diffhunk://#diff-0a16a9b3407731d626139acc756b92c6edd5cede97b04445f9fa3d5a2b0ebe63L5-R6) [[8]](diffhunk://#diff-9dfae7f2b08fd26b01e9419f9030d86ae864d345d9a08812b140589943b52eb7L4)

* Split combined import statements for clarity in `app.py` and ensured consistent import style.

**Formatting and code clarity:**

* Removed unnecessary use of f-strings in static markdown content in `app.py` and ensured proper HTML rendering. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-568470d013cd12e4f388206520da39ab9a4e4c3c6b95846cbc281abc1ba3c959L610-R610) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-568470d013cd12e4f388206520da39ab9a4e4c3c6b95846cbc281abc1ba3c959L674-R675)

* Improved conditional statement formatting for readability in `llm_data/generate_instructions.py`.

**Test improvements:**

* Added `# noqa: F401` comments to certain imports (such as `faiss` and `SentenceTransformer`) in `tests/test_retriever.py` to suppress linter warnings about unused imports when those imports are needed only for their side effects. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-0a16a9b3407731d626139acc756b92c6edd5cede97b04445f9fa3d5a2b0ebe63L79-R78) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-0a16a9b3407731d626139acc756b92c6edd5cede97b04445f9fa3d5a2b0ebe63L104-R104) [[3]](diffhunk://#diff-0a16a9b3407731d626139acc756b92c6edd5cede97b04445f9fa3d5a2b0ebe63L181-R180)

**Minor logic and string changes:**

* Standardized string formatting in maintenance recommendation generation by removing unnecessary f-strings when no interpolation is needed in `llm_data/enrich_text.py` and `llm_data/generate_text.py`.

**Miscellaneous:**

* Removed an unused import from `fine_tuning/lora_setup.py` for a cleaner script.…ed readability and maintainability

prs.title: Add unit tests for API endpoints, chunking, memory, retriever, and Ve…
prs.body:
This pull request introduces a comprehensive testing framework and continuous integration (CI) setup for the Vehicle Maintenance Prediction project. It adds automated workflows for testing, linting, and type checking, and provides extensive pytest-based unit and integration tests for the FastAPI API endpoints. The changes ensure robust validation, coverage, and maintainability of the codebase.

**Continuous Integration and Quality Assurance:**

* Added a GitHub Actions workflow (`.github/workflows/ci.yml`) to automate testing across multiple Python versions (3.10, 3.11, 3.12), run linting with Ruff, and perform type checking with mypy. The workflow also uploads coverage reports to Codecov.
* Introduced a `pytest.ini` configuration for consistent test discovery, verbosity, and warning filtering.

**Testing Infrastructure:**

* Added `tests/conftest.py` with reusable pytest fixtures for sample data, conversation history, and temporary directories, facilitating easier and more maintainable test writing.
* Created an empty `tests/__init__.py` to mark the test directory as a package.

**API Endpoint Testing:**

* Implemented `tests/test_api.py` with extensive unit and integration tests for all major FastAPI endpoints (`/health`, `/api/chat`, `/api/retrieve`, `/api/predict`). The tests cover normal operation, edge cases, validation errors, and various input scenarios, ensuring high reliability and correctness of the API

prs.title: feat: Complete ML pipeline, model-serving API, RAG retrieval, and AI chatbot
prs.body:
End-to-end implementation of the Vehicle Maintenance Prediction system — from model training and evaluation in the notebook through to a production-ready FastAPI backend with real ML inference, semantic RAG retrieval, and a conversational AI chatbot.

This pull request introduces major improvements to the AI chatbot system, focusing on three main areas: a robust conversational AI agent with memory and multi-backend support, a production-grade ML risk prediction API, and enhanced request/response models. The changes include a new `ConversationMemory` module, a complete rewrite of `VehicleAI` to support RAG and LLM APIs with fallback logic, and a new ML prediction pipeline with proper feature engineering and one-hot encoding.

**Conversational AI and Memory Enhancements:**

- Introduced `ConversationMemory` in `chatbot/memory.py`, providing a configurable sliding window for chat history, formatted context retrieval, and memory clearing.
- Rewrote `VehicleAI` in `chatbot/vehicle_ai.py` to support:
  - RAG retrieval (with lazy initialization and fallback if unavailable)
  - LLM API integration (Groq and HuggingFace backends, auto-detected via environment variables)
  - Smart rule-based fallback if no API key is present
  - Context-aware prompt building using chat memory and retrieved knowledge

**ML Risk Prediction Pipeline:**

- Added model loading and feature column management in `main.py`, loading XGBoost model and required columns at startup.
- Overhauled the `/api/predict` endpoint to:
  - Engineer features (including interactions and one-hot encoding) to match the training pipeline
  - Predict maintenance risk using the loaded XGBoost model, returning probability, risk score, and actionable recommendations based on risk thresholds

**API Model and Endpoint Improvements:**

- Expanded `PredictRequest` in `main.py` to include all necessary features for ML prediction, with type hints and descriptions for each field.
- Improved lazy initialization and sharing of the RAG retriever between endpoints for resource efficiency.

**Other Improvements:**

- Added `.vscode/settings.json` for Python project configuration.
- Cleaned up imports and error handling throughout `main.py` for robustness and maintainability.

These changes collectively make the backend ready for production use, with robust AI/ML capabilities and clear, maintainable code.

prs.title: backend improve
prs.body:
This pull request introduces a comprehensive overhaul to the club management backend, adding full-featured REST API endpoints for users, projects, events, attendance, and tasks. It implements new serializers for detailed and minimal representations, robust filtering and permission logic, and supports actions such as joining/leaving projects, marking/verifying attendance, and submitting/verifying tasks. The changes also restructure the API URLs for better organization and expand user-related functionality with registration, login, profile management, and leaderboard endpoints.

**API Endpoint Expansion & Routing**

* Added new REST API endpoints for `projects`, `events`, `attendance`, and `tasks` using Django REST Framework viewsets and routers in `server/club/urls.py` and `server/users/urls.py`, enabling CRUD operations and custom actions for each resource. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-e0616990eebdff6cdab21cd27607e232df56df4766ee01dcc5551f5ca8a1dfe8L3-R16) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-0a5682efeaae04b54747f52b8bc4259a58e9c8ca38bd797ee7e4d6f8abacf1bcL1-R17)

**Serializers & Data Representation**

* Introduced multiple new serializers in `server/club/serializers.py` and `server/users/serializers.py` for minimal and detailed user info, project/task/event creation/updating, attendance marking, and leaderboard display, supporting nested representations and computed fields. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-3ed5db9bfd9e3de4ccfe6d085c62afea1b070b4615c5863f2d7c8b143420ae3eR2-R125) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-a96900f1d79dd88262bd75e422c800dbe0c4f1fb06c8d1150848bf0cbfb0b24dR6-R72)

**ViewSets & Business Logic**

* Implemented viewsets for `Project`, `Event`, `Attendance`, and `Task` in `server/club/views.py` with advanced filtering, permission checks (including admin-only actions), and custom endpoints for joining/leaving projects, marking bulk attendance, submitting/verifying tasks, and retrieving attendees.

**User Management Features**

* Added user registration, login, logout, profile update, and leaderboard endpoints with corresponding serializers and views, supporting password validation, profile editing, and leaderboard ranking. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-a96900f1d79dd88262bd75e422c800dbe0c4f1fb06c8d1150848bf0cbfb0b24dR6-R72) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-0a5682efeaae04b54747f52b8bc4259a58e9c8ca38bd797ee7e4d6f8abacf1bcL1-R17)

**Dashboard & Aggregated Data**

* Enhanced the dashboard endpoint to include user's active tasks, upcoming events, recent projects, attendance count, and extended user profile info, improving the overview for authenticated users.

Let me know if you need clarification on any specific endpoint, serializer, or permission logic!

issues.title: [FEATURE] Add AI-powered database schema generation to DrawDB, allowing users to describe their database in natural language and automatically generate tables, fields, and relationships.
issues.body:
## Feature request 
Designing database schemas manually is time-consuming and error-prone. Users need to quickly prototype schemas but manually creating tables, fields, and relationships slows down the ideation process.

## The solution 
An AI-powered feature that generates complete database schemas from natural language descriptions (e.g., "Create a social media platform with users, posts, and comments"). The system automatically generates tables, fields with correct data types, primary/foreign keys, and relationships. Supports multiple AI providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google AI, Groq) for flexibility.

## Summary
This feature integrates multiple AI providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google AI, Groq) to generate database schemas from natural language descriptions. Users can describe their database requirements in plain English, and the AI will generate a complete schema with properly structured tables, fields, and relationships.

issues.title: Setup REST API for Proposals
issues.body:
### **Create RESTful API endpoints in the Django backend (server/gsoc_review) to manage student proposals. This includes creating, retrieving, updating, and deleting proposals. The API will serve as the backend for the React frontend, allowing students to submit proposals and mentors/admins to review them.**

> **Endpoint: _GET /proposals/_ → fetch all proposals
> Endpoint: _POST /proposals/_ → submit a proposal
> Endpoint: _GET /proposals/:id/_ → get proposal details**

issues.title: [PERF]  Make  User Interface (UI) interactive
issues.body: /--- ## Description The current user interface (UI) feels static and lacks interactivity across various platforms (Web, Android, iOS, and Expo). This issue aims to enhance user engagement by introducing smoother transitions, responsive animations, and overall improved user experience. The goal is to make every element feel fluid and dynamic while maintaining performance and accessibility. --- ## Objectives - Improve responsiveness and interaction feedback (buttons, modals, inputs). - Implement lightweight, high-performance animations and transitions. - Ensure consistency across all clients (Android, iOS, Web, Expo). - Optimize rendering performance for smoother navigation and faster UI loading times. - Maintain accessibility standards (focus states, ARIA roles, etc.). --- ## Scope of Work | Platform | Framework | Task | |-----------|------------|------| | **Web** | Vue.js | Add transitions for components, improve layout responsiveness | | **Android** | Kotlin | Implement material motion, smooth scrolling, and view animations | | **iOS** | SwiftUI | Add interactive transitions and reduce frame drops | | **Expo** | React Native | Add gesture-based interactions and improve screen transitions | --- ## Technical Notes - Ensure minimal frame drops (<16ms render time). - Use efficient animation libraries (e.g., MotionLayout for Android, Lottie for cross-platform). - Reuse design tokens (colors, typography, spacing) from the global theme configuration. - Profile UI performance using native profiling tools and browser DevTools. --- ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] UI elements respond smoothly to user input. - [ ] Animation performance is optimized on all platforms. - [ ] No significant increase in memory or CPU usage. - [ ] Design consistency is maintained across devices. - [ ] Accessibility and responsiveness verified. --- ## Additional Context Please specify: - Platform (Web / Android / iOS / Expo) - Current UI component or screen - Description of existing behavior vs expected interactive behavior - Screenshots or video (if applicable) --- ## Assigned Developers - Open for contributors --- ## Priority **High** — Improves user engagement and usability across all platforms.

issues.title: GitHub API Integration
issues.body:
### **Fetch Pull Requests for a given GitHub username.**
 **Filter contributions by:**

- [ ] Username (GitHub handle)

- [ ] Date range (Hacktoberfest: Oct 1 – Oct 31)

**Contribution type (issues / PRs).**

 

- [ ] Implement authentication using a GitHub Personal Access Token (PAT).

- [ ] PAT should be stored securely in the Go backend.

 **Create backend routes in Go (using Gofr):**

`/api/github/issues?user=USERNAME
`
`/api/github/prs?user=USERNAME`

 **Backend should:**

- [ ] Fetch data from GitHub API.

- [ ] Process & store contributions in the database.


- [ ] Return JSON response to the frontend.

issues.title: Overlapping Design and Misaligned Elements on Interactive DNR Tool Page
issues.body: ## Expected Behavior The layout of the "Interactive DNR Tool" page should have a clean, non-overlapping design where all elements are properly aligned. The navigation menu, content sections, and input areas should be displayed without any visual conflicts, ensuring a responsive and user-friendly interface. ## Actual Behavior - The "Upload Extension Files" and "Upload Manifest" sections overlap each other, making the interface cluttered and difficult to use. - The Chrome Labs logo also appears to be incorrectly positioned, contributing to the misalignment. ## Steps to Reproduce the Problem 1. Open the webpage displaying the "Interactive DNR Tool." 2. Observe the layout on a screen resolution of 768 x 1009 or similar. 3. Note the overlapping content in the middle of the page. ## Specifications - Version: [Specify the version of the application or webpage if known] - Platform: macOS or any browser (tested with Chrome as shown in the screenshot) - Screen Resolution: 768 x 1009 - Additional details: Refer to the attached screenshot for visual reference. ## Suggested Fix - Adjust the CSS `position` and `margin` properties for the "Upload Extension Files" and "Upload Manifest" sections to prevent overlapping. - Ensure proper `flexbox` or `grid` layout usage to maintain responsiveness. - Correct the positioning of the Chrome Labs logo to align with its intended placement.
